meta.study_type: short mechanistic 2-arm x 3-diagnosis-group trial (VitD3 4000 IU/day vs placebo, 21 days); endpoints are change-from-baseline biomarker deltas, no time-to-event
meta.priority_hierarchy: (1) ctgov results JSON (tier1) -> (2) foundation-model sanity defaults (tier2) -> (3) paperclip literature priors (tier3, via literature-search-paperclip skill)
calibrated_features: baseline population marginals (diagnosis group, sex, race, ethnicity, region, age, BMI, Fitzpatrick)
baseline serum labs (vitamin D, calcium, creatinine, PTH, IgE)
primary endpoint change: CAMP mRNA lesional & non-lesional (per group x arm)
primary endpoint change: HBD-3 mRNA lesional & non-lesional (per group x arm)
secondary endpoint change: IL-13 mRNA lesional & non-lesional (per group x arm)
adverse-event burden (calibrates to ZERO: no AE >=5%, no SAE)
disposition (6/82 not completed; reasons WD 3 / PV 2 / AE 1)
uncalibrated_features_target_null: serum vitamin D at Day 21 (mediator; paperclip S7/S9 prior)
baseline absolute cutaneous AMP/TH2/CFU levels (only the CHANGE was posted)
all latent frailty variances (model; never zeroed per invariant #4)
meta.note: Uncalibrated features are still simulated from cited priors and still obey every DAG gate; they simply have no posted marginal to tune in Step 6 and are NOT validated against this trial.

S1.quote: the cathelicidin promoter had three VDREs ... VDR activation was required for expression of both antimicrobial genes (CAMP and DEFB4)

S2.quote: A deficiency in the expression of antimicrobial peptides may account for the susceptibility of patients with atopic dermatitis to skin infection with S. aureus.

S3.cite: Szabo et al., Acta Derm Venereol 2023
S3.quote: LL-37 was the only antimicrobial peptide that was impaired in both non-lesional and lesional atopic dermatitis

S4.cite: Kim et al., J Korean Med Sci 2005
S4.quote: Defensins and cathelicidins (LL-37) ... their expression can be markedly increased in inflammatory skin disease such as psoriasis

S5.quote: IL-4 and IL-13 Negatively Regulate ... beta-Defensin Expression

S6.cite: Hata et al., JEADV 2014 (this trial's main-study RCT publication, PMID 23638978)
S6.quote: darker skin types and elevated BMI are important risk factors for vitamin D deficiency in subjects with AD

S7.quote: Oral vitamin D3 supplementation significantly increases serum vitamin D levels. This relationship is dose-dependent, with a strong correlation observed across numerous studies.

S8.quote: Does the severity of atopic dermatitis correlate with serum IgE levels? (serum IgE tracks AD severity / atopy)

S9.cite: Kralova et al., In Vivo 2024 (abstract corpus) - 1000/2000/4000/8000 IU in young healthy adults
S9.quote: Effective vitamin D supplementation in healthy young adults can be achieved with a daily dose of 2,000 IU during winter. However, 4,000 IU/day was more effective for maintaining levels above 100 nmol/[L].

p_race.evidence: White 54, Asian 10, Black 9, Other 9 (American Indian 0, Native Hawaiian/PI 0, >1 race 0)

p_hispanic.evidence: Hispanic or Latino 9; Not Hispanic or Latino 73

p_fitzpatrick.evidence: Extremely Fair 3, Fair 20, Medium 26, Olive 22, Moderately Brown 4, Markedly Black 6, Unknown 1

age_by_group.evidence: arm-pooled per diagnosis group (arm-independent by randomization); Total 32.5 (SD 10.9); Psoriasis highest (40.5/37.1)

bmi_by_group.evidence: arm-pooled per group; Total 25.3 (SD 4.9); Psoriasis higher (28.0/27.1)

height_by_sex_cm.evidence: adult height mean/SD by sex; not posted - weight back-derived so BMI matches the posted marginal

psor_duration_min_mo.evidence: Definitive diagnosis of typical plaque psoriasis for at least 6 months

psor_duration_excess_mean_mo.evidence: 6 + Exp(mean~72) months for psoriasis subjects; duration not posted (uncalibrated)

psor_severity_probs.evidence: severity mild/moderate/severe reported; distribution is a prior (not posted) - drives baseline PASI

mh_logit_intercept.evidence: healthy population (Diabetes / kidney disease / autoimmune all excluded) -> sparse MH; P(item)=sigma(-2.5+0.03*(age-40))

mh_age_slope.evidence: mild age gradient on medical-history item prevalence (assumption)

vitd_bl_darkskin_coef.evidence: darker skin types and elevated BMI are important risk factors for vitamin D deficiency in subjects with AD

vitd_bl_bmi_coef.evidence: vitamin D falls with higher BMI - darker skin types and elevated BMI are important risk factors for vitamin D deficiency in subjects with AD

calcium_bl_by_group.evidence: Total 9.4 (SD 0.4) mg/dL; eligibility requires within normal limits (8.4-10.2)

calcium_clip.evidence: screening calcium within normal limits (exclusion if not)

pth_vitd_coupling.evidence: physiologic inverse vitamin-D<->PTH coupling (PTH falls as vitD rises); assumption

ige_bl_lognormal_by_group.source: ctgov: baselineCharacteristicsModule (Total Serum IgE) + paperclip: S8
ige_bl_lognormal_by_group.evidence: arm-pooled raw mean AD 1263.8 vs Non-AD 57.0 vs Psor 77.4 kU/L -> [median, sigma] by method-of-moments; Does the severity of atopic dermatitis correlate with serum IgE levels? (serum IgE tracks AD severity / atopy)

rast_logit.evidence: RAST positivity ~ atopy/IgE; Bern(sigma(-1.5+1.2*1[AD]+0.6*z(logIgE))); NO posted result -> UNCALIBRATED

serumstor_destroy_if_screenfail.evidence: If a subject screen-fails at V1 on Ca/PTH/creatinine, remaining serum is destroyed and NOT analyzed for IgE or RAST

camp_bl_intercept_by_group.evidence: baseline cathelicidin ordering alpha_Psor>alpha_NonAD>alpha_AD; Defensins and cathelicidins (LL-37) ... their expression can be markedly increased in inflammatory skin disease such as psoriasis / LL-37 was the only antimicrobial peptide that was impaired in both non-lesional and lesional atopic dermatitis

camp_bl_lesional_offset.evidence: lesional > non-lesional AMP in inflammatory skin disease; Defensins and cathelicidins (LL-37) ... their expression can be markedly increased in inflammatory skin disease such as psoriasis

camp_th2_suppression_coef.evidence: TH2 (IL-4/IL-13) suppresses AMP; IL-4 and IL-13 Negatively Regulate ... beta-Defensin Expression

hbd3_bl_intercept_by_group.evidence: HBD-3 baseline ordering as cathelicidin; Defensins and cathelicidins (LL-37) ... their expression can be markedly increased in inflammatory skin disease such as psoriasis

th2_bl_intercept_by_group.evidence: IL-13/IL-4 baseline highest in AD (TH2 disease); IL-4 and IL-13 Negatively Regulate ... beta-Defensin Expression

sal_amp_beta1.evidence: saliva AMP reflects the same VitD-AMP axis, normalized to total protein; qRT-PCR for cathelicidin mRNA - UNCALIBRATED

sal_totprot_mean_sd.evidence: total protein by BCA; saliva AMP normalizer - UNCALIBRATED

ts_amp_gamma.evidence: tape-strip AMP estimates biopsy (gold standard) via Pearson correlation - UNCALIBRATED

cfu_bl_intercept_by_group.evidence: log CFU baseline highest in AD (AMP-deficient); A deficiency in the expression of antimicrobial peptides may account for the susceptibility of patients with atopic dermatitis to skin infection with S. aureus.

cfu_amp_coef.evidence: lower AMP -> higher colony counts; A deficiency in the expression of antimicrobial peptides may account for the susceptibility of patients with atopic dermatitis to skin infection with S. aureus.

sw_flora_collect_min_per_group.evidence: skin swabs collected on a subset (>=6 per diagnostic group), stored for future genomic analysis - UNCALIBRATED

substrate_noise_sd.evidence: residual epsilon SD on baseline cutaneous readouts (assumption)

sigma_f_amp.evidence: shared cutaneous AMP tone; correlates CAMP/HBD3 across lesional, non-lesional, saliva, tape-strip (the primary contrast is within-subject -> MUST correlate)

sigma_f_th2.evidence: shared TH2 tone; correlates IL-13/IL-4 and inversely suppresses AMP

sigma_f_vitd_resp.evidence: per-patient vitamin-D responsiveness; correlates treatment-driven AMP change across a subject's readouts and supplies the VitD-arm excess change variance

sigma_f_microbiome.evidence: shared colonization propensity; correlates lesional/non-lesional CFU and swab flora

sigma_f_ae.evidence: procedural/GI AE propensity; correlates the (rare) AEs within patient (no independent-draw bug)

p_arm_vitd.evidence: allocation RANDOMIZED, 1:1 to Vitamin D3 vs placebo within each diagnostic group; Bern(0.5) exogenous

ex_dose_iu.evidence: oral vitamin D3 (cholecalciferol) 4,000 IU/day for 21 days (24 capsules dispensed); route ORAL, frequency QD

ex_return_logit_intercept.evidence: return prob q=sigma(-2.4+0.9*f_dropout); EXNRET~Binom(24,q); unused medication returned, pill counts at V3

ex_return_dropout_coef.evidence: higher dropout frailty -> more capsules returned (worse compliance)

vitd_d21_delta_full_compliance.evidence: serum 25-OH-D rise (ng/mL) at Day 21 under 4000 IU/day at full compliance (scaled by EXCOMPL/100). Effective vitamin D supplementation in healthy young adults can be achieved with a daily dose of 2,000 IU during winter. However, 4,000 IU/day was more effective for maintaining levels above 100 nmol/[L]. [Jarrett et al., 2014 review: dose-dependent rise]. NOT posted at Day 21 -> UNCALIBRATED

vitd_d21_resp_coef.evidence: eta on f_vitd_resp -> per-patient spread of the serum-vitD rise

vitd_d21_noise_sd.evidence: residual SD on Day-21 serum vitamin D (assumption)

calcium_d21_ar.evidence: AR(1) toward 9.4; mild VitD3 calcium bump +0.05, clip 8.4-10.6 - UNCALIBRATED

calcium_d21_vitd_bump.evidence: VitD3 raises calcium mildly (physiologic); calcium graded per Appendix II

creat_d21_ar.evidence: renal function stable over 21 days - UNCALIBRATED

pth_d21_ar.evidence: PTH AR(1); falls as vitamin D rises (coupling -0.15*deltaVITD) - UNCALIBRATED

vitals_ar.evidence: vital signs AR(1) around age-appropriate norms; no drug effect expected; safety/shift tables - UNCALIBRATED

pe_abnormal_skin_prob_by_group.evidence: physical/oral exam mostly normal; abnormal-skin flag driven by diagnosis group - UNCALIBRATED

pasi_by_severity.evidence: baseline PASI by psoriasis severity (psoriasis only); VitD3 effects on PASI change analyzed but NO posted result -> UNCALIBRATED

pasi_vitd_delta.evidence: small PASI reduction under VitD3 over 21 days (oral vitD has weak short-term plaque effect); prior only - UNCALIBRATED

lesional.evidence: CAMP mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), AD lesional: VitD3 -0.4 (SD 2.8) vs placebo 0.1 (SD 2.4), ANOVA p=0.7

nonlesional.evidence: CAMP mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), AD nonlesional: VitD3 -0.6 (SD 2.0) vs placebo 0.7 (SD 2.5), ANOVA p=0.12
CAMP mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), NonAD nonlesional: VitD3 1.2 (SD 2.4) vs placebo 0.3 (SD 1.7), ANOVA p=0.3

lesional.evidence: CAMP mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), Psor lesional: VitD3 -0.9 (SD 3.0) vs placebo 0.2 (SD 3.2), ANOVA p=0.4

nonlesional.evidence: CAMP mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), Psor nonlesional: VitD3 -0.6 (SD 2.3) vs placebo 0.7 (SD 2.4), ANOVA p=0.2

lesional.evidence: HBD3 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), AD lesional: VitD3 -0.1 (SD 7.3) vs placebo -0.8 (SD 2.8), ANOVA p=0.8

nonlesional.evidence: HBD3 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), AD nonlesional: VitD3 -0.1 (SD 3.5) vs placebo 1.4 (SD 3.9), ANOVA p=0.2
HBD3 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), NonAD nonlesional: VitD3 0.2 (SD 2.8) vs placebo 1.2 (SD 3.2), ANOVA p=0.4

lesional.evidence: HBD3 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), Psor lesional: VitD3 0.8 (SD 5.3) vs placebo -1.6 (SD 4.4), ANOVA p=0.4

nonlesional.evidence: HBD3 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (PRIMARY), Psor nonlesional: VitD3 -1.5 (SD 3.2) vs placebo -1.5 (SD 4.3), ANOVA p=1.0

lesional.evidence: IL13 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (SECONDARY), AD lesional: VitD3 -0.7 (SD 2.8) vs placebo 1.1 (SD 2.7), ANOVA p=0.2

nonlesional.evidence: IL13 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (SECONDARY), AD nonlesional: VitD3 -0.8 (SD 2.3) vs placebo -0.1 (SD 3.2), ANOVA p=0.5
IL13 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (SECONDARY), NonAD nonlesional: VitD3 0.2 (SD 2.4) vs placebo 0.5 (SD 1.5), ANOVA p=0.7

lesional.evidence: IL13 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (SECONDARY), Psor lesional: VitD3 -2.1 (SD 4.6) vs placebo -0.1 (SD 4.1), ANOVA p=0.2

nonlesional.evidence: IL13 mRNA change baseline->Day21 (SECONDARY), Psor nonlesional: VitD3 -0.3 (SD 2.7) vs placebo 0.9 (SD 3.3), ANOVA p=0.3

il4_change_vitd_delta.evidence: IL-4 change (TH2) collected per objectives but only IL-13 posted -> UNCALIBRATED; VitD modestly lowers TH2; IL-4 and IL-13 Negatively Regulate ... beta-Defensin Expression

il4_change_sd.evidence: IL-4 change SD prior (mirrors IL-13 scale) - UNCALIBRATED

sal_amp_vitd_delta.evidence: saliva CAMP/HBD3 change under VitD (same induction term as biopsy); the cathelicidin promoter had three VDREs ... VDR activation was required for expression of both antimicrobial genes (CAMP and DEFB4) - UNCALIBRATED

ts_amp_vitd_delta.evidence: tape-strip CAMP/HBD3 change tracks biopsy (Pearson vs gold-standard biopsy) - UNCALIBRATED

cfu_d21_ar.evidence: log CFU AR(1); higher AMP change -> lower CFU (rho); A deficiency in the expression of antimicrobial peptides may account for the susceptibility of patients with atopic dermatitis to skin infection with S. aureus. - UNCALIBRATED

cfu_amp_change_coef.evidence: rho: AMP increase lowers colony counts - UNCALIBRATED

ige_d21_ar.evidence: serum IgE stable over 21 days (small VitD effect); IgE/RAST change collected but NOT posted -> UNCALIBRATED

rast_d21_flip_prob.evidence: RAST rarely changes over 21 days - UNCALIBRATED

sw_flora_change_prob.evidence: swab flora profile change baseline->Day21, stored for future genomic analysis - UNCALIBRATED

ae_base_haz_vd_gi.evidence: per-visit hazard of VitD3 GI AE (constipation/gas/bloating). 'There were no serious adverse events. No adverse events occurred at a 5% or greater frequency threshold.' -> tune very low so nothing reaches 5%

ae_base_haz_procedural.evidence: per-visit hazard of procedural AE (blood-draw pain/bruising, tape mild transient erythema, skin biopsy pain/swelling/bleeding). Calibrate to zero (<5%)

ae_rr_vd.evidence: VitD3 arm relative risk on GI AE class (expected AEs constipation, gas, bloating)

ae_p_severe.evidence: P(severe) underlying draw; AESER derived (serious only if grade 4 - none observed)

fda_grade_thresholds.value: FDA Toxicity Grading Scale 1-4 (deterministic; NEVER tuned)
fda_grade_thresholds.evidence: Severity graded 1-4 (FDA Toxicity Grading Scale, Sept 2007): Grade 1 mild, 2 moderate, 3 severe, 4 life-threatening/death

ae_relatedness_scale.evidence: Relationship/attribution to study drug: 1 Unrelated, 2 Unlikely, 3 Possible, 4 Probable, 5 Definite

disc_logit_intercept.evidence: P(not completed)=sigma(-2.6+0.8*f_dropout+1.2*1[drug not returned by Day27]); 6/82 (7.3%) did not complete

disc_drugreturn_coef.evidence: not returning drug by Day 27 => discontinued and replaced

disc_reason_probs.evidence: dropWithdraw reasons: Withdrawal by Subject 3, Protocol Violation 2, Adverse Event 1

site_single_center.evidence: PROTOCOL CHAIR Richard Gallo, MD, PhD (UCSD); single-center -> SITEID ~ Uniform(UCSD sites)

photo_taken.evidence: photographs of lesional skin at V2 and V3 -> BX.PHOTO = 'taken' (deterministic)

preg_result.evidence: females of childbearing potential only; pregnancy is an exclusion -> PGRESULT = negative (deterministic)

cm_prevalence.evidence: concomitant meds monitored; many classes withheld/excluded per 9.4 -> sparse con-med prevalence; UNCALIBRATED

swcollect_prob.evidence: SWCOLLECT (subset >=6/group), SWLOC in {lesional, non-lesional}; stored for future analysis; UNCALIBRATED

change_rule.value: endpoint = value(Day21) - value(baseline); primary contrast = delta_lesional - delta_nonlesional
change_rule.evidence: every endpoint is a change-from-baseline of a Layer-Lt node (invariant #1: never drawn directly); primary contrast is within-subject lesional-change minus non-lesional-change

efficacy_population_rule.value: randomized WITH both a baseline AND a Day-21 AMP biopsy
efficacy_population_rule.evidence: Efficacy population = randomized with BOTH a baseline AND a day-21 AMP biopsy assessment
